 {"id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","title":"Compat persistence: real SQLite file read/write (no dump shim)","description":"## Summary
Ensure `fsqlite::Connection::open(path)` can operate on **real SQLite-format files** end-to-end (not a SQL-dump persistence shim).

## Context
README notes that current runnable backend may persist non-:memory: DBs by dumping/reloading SQL. The RealDB E2E suite needs true file-format I/O.

## Requirements
- Open an existing `.db` file and read schema/data correctly.
- Apply DDL/DML and persist changes to the file format.
- Closing and reopening must preserve state.

## Acceptance
- A simple harness test: open a real file, create table, insert rows, close, reopen, query rows.
- Output DB readable by `sqlite3` (at least for the written subset).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.010884845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:21.487900875Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:21.487880367Z","close_reason":"Real SQLite binary format persistence via pager+btree stack. compat_persist module with persist_to_sqlite/load_from_sqlite. 10 tests + 91 existing connection tests pass. Legacy text dump fallback preserved. Commit b30fc29.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","e2e","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.010884845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:18.258554202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3qrk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:19.108127550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1074,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1w6k.2.3","title":"Expose determinism + durability knobs (PRAGMAs / API) for harness","description":"## Summary
Expose/standardize the knobs the harness will need to control determinism and fairness.

## Required knobs
- journal mode / WAL mode
- synchronous setting
- cache_size
- page_size for newly created DBs / canonicalization outputs
- MVCC/concurrent mode toggle (if available)

## Acceptance
- Harness can set these consistently for both sqlite3 and FrankenSQLite runs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.240572095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:06:58.178627489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","e2e","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.240572095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:18.471111201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1075,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-1w6k.2.4","title":"Public MVCC toggle for harness (BEGIN CONCURRENT / PRAGMA)","description":"## Summary
Expose a clear way for the harness to request concurrent-writer mode (MVCC) for performance demos.

## Requirements
- API or PRAGMA to enable concurrency mode.
- Document expected transient errors and retry semantics (BUSY, BUSY_SNAPSHOT).
- Ensure mode selection is captured in run reports.

## Acceptance
- Harness can run the same workload in single-writer vs MVCC mode and observe different concurrency behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.470867063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:31:04.013330884Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:31:04.013311518Z","close_reason":"Implemented MVCC concurrent mode toggle: PRAGMA concurrent_mode=ON/OFF, BEGIN CONCURRENT support, public accessors, 10 tests passing, all quality gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mvcc","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.470867063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:18.683588660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1076,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:32Z"},{"id":1170,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-1w6k.2.4 — Public MVCC toggle for harness (BEGIN CONCURRENT / PRAGMA) — COMPLETE

### Changes Made

**crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs:**
- Added `concurrent_txn: RefCell<bool>` and `concurrent_mode_default: RefCell<bool>` fields to `Connection`
- `execute_begin()` now respects `BeginStatement.mode` — `BEGIN CONCURRENT` sets the concurrent flag; plain `BEGIN` is promoted when `concurrent_mode_default` is ON
- `execute_commit()` and `execute_rollback()` clear the concurrent flag
- Added `Statement::Pragma` handling in `execute_statement()` routing to new `execute_pragma()`
- `execute_pragma()` handles `PRAGMA concurrent_mode=ON/OFF` and `PRAGMA fsqlite.concurrent_mode=ON/OFF`
- Public accessors: `is_concurrent_transaction()`, `is_concurrent_mode_default()`
- `parse_pragma_bool()` free function: handles Literal::True/False (parser converts ON→True), Integer 0/1, String, and identifier column refs (OFF parsed as identifier)

**crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/fsqlite_executor.rs:**
- Added `concurrent_mode: bool` field to `FsqliteExecConfig`
- `run_oplog_fsqlite()` injects `PRAGMA concurrent_mode=ON` before user pragmas when enabled
- `build_report()` records mode in notes: "mode=concurrent (MVCC)" or "mode=single-writer (serialized)"

**crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs (pre-existing fix):**
- Removed unnecessary `ref` bindings at lines 1505/1519 (Edition 2024 implicit borrowing)

### Tests Added (10 new, all passing)
1. `test_concurrent_mode_default_off` — default is OFF
2. `test_pragma_concurrent_mode_on_off` — ON/OFF toggling
3. `test_pragma_concurrent_mode_qualified_name` — `fsqlite.concurrent_mode`
4. `test_pragma_concurrent_mode_integer_values` — 1/0 values
5. `test_begin_concurrent_sets_flag` — explicit BEGIN CONCURRENT
6. `test_begin_promoted_to_concurrent_by_pragma` — plain BEGIN promoted when pragma ON
7. `test_explicit_mode_overrides_pragma_default` — DEFERRED overrides pragma
8. `test_rollback_clears_concurrent_flag` — cleanup on rollback
9. `test_concurrent_mode_with_single_vs_mvcc_workload` — end-to-end sequence
10. `test_unrecognised_pragma_silently_ignored` — unknown pragmas don't error

### Quality Gates
- cargo check --workspace: PASS
- cargo clippy (pedantic+nursery): PASS
- cargo fmt: PASS
- cargo doc: PASS (1 pre-existing warning in fsqlite-e2e)
- 659 tests in fsqlite-core (10 new, all pass)
","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:30:58Z"}]}

Expose a hook/flag for enabling WAL-FEC recovery so the corruption demo can explicitly show “repair on open”.

## Requirements
- A clear user-facing switch (PRAGMA or open option).
- Recovery logs surfaced in a structured way (tracing events).

## Acceptance
- Corruption harness can run 2 cases: recovery OFF (expect failure) vs recovery ON (expect repair success when symbols sufficient).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.696530747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:11:08.545588452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","e2e","raptorq","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.696530747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:18.894712285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1077,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:32Z"},{"id":1171,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.2.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-1w6k.2.5 — Implementation Complete (ScarletElk)

### Summary
Added recovery toggle and structured logging surface for WAL-FEC demos.

### Changes

**crates/fsqlite-wal/src/wal_fec.rs:**
- Added `RecoveryDisabled` variant to `WalFecRecoveryFallbackReason`
- Added `WalFecRecoveryConfig` struct with `recovery_enabled: bool` (default true)
- Added `WalFecRecoveryLog` struct with 12 structured fields for observability
- Added `recover_wal_fec_group_with_config()` — config-aware wrapper
- Added `recovery_log_from_outcome()` — log extraction helper

**crates/fsqlite-wal/src/lib.rs:**
- Exported new types and functions

**crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/recovery_demo.rs:**
- Added `RecoveryDemoConfig` struct with `recovery_enabled` and `repair_symbols`
- Added `recovery_log` field to `RecoveryDemoResult`
- Added `attempt_wal_fec_recovery_with_config()` public function
- Made helper functions and types public for downstream use
- Fixed WAL persistence issue with reader lock pattern
- Fixed pre-existing compile errors (module was never compiled before)
- 3 new tests: disabled recovery, enabled with config log, config default

**crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/lib.rs:**
- Added `pub mod recovery_demo;` (was missing — module was never compiled)
- Fixed `unused_mut` in `to_fsqlite_pragmas`

**crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/sqlite_executor.rs:**
- Fixed pre-existing `too_many_lines` and `manual_let_else` clippy errors

### Quality Gates
- 9/9 recovery_demo tests passing
- 131/131 fsqlite-wal tests passing
- clippy pedantic+nursery clean
- cargo fmt clean
","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:38:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1w6k.3","title":"Harness: RealDB E2E Runner (sqlite3 vs fsqlite)","description":"Build the Rust harness/CLI that runs the E2E demos:
- baseline engine: `sqlite3` (oracle)
- candidate engine: FrankenSQLite (`fsqlite`)

This harness orchestrates:
- copying golden DBs into per-run working dirs
- applying workloads (deterministic or concurrent)
- validation + canonicalization + hashing
- benchmarking + reporting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.879741311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.879741311Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","e2e","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.879741311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1w6k.3.1","title":"Harness skeleton: choose location + define CLI","description":"## Summary
Decide where the RealDB E2E runner lives and define its CLI contract.

## Placement options
- Add a new subcommand/binary under `crates/fsqlite-harness/` (preferred: avoids new crate)
- Add a new `tools/` Rust binary (only if harness crate layering makes it painful)

## CLI contract (sketch)
- `realdb-e2e corpus scan|import|verify`
- `realdb-e2e run --engine sqlite3|fsqlite --db <db_id> --workload <name> --concurrency <n>`
- `realdb-e2e bench ...`
- `realdb-e2e corrupt ...`

## Acceptance
- We have a decided location + top-level help output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.926043800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:43:02.081819273Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:43:02.081797893Z","close_reason":"Harness skeleton decided: realdb-e2e binary in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs. CLI contract: corpus scan|import|verify, run --engine --db --workload --concurrency, bench, corrupt. Full help output implemented. corpus scan wired to fixture_discovery. 11 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","harness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:20.926043800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1078,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1w6k.3.2","title":"Baseline driver: run workloads via sqlite3 (oracle)","description":"## Summary
Implement the baseline/oracle executor for “regular SQLite”.

## Requirement
Use a well-defined `sqlite3` version and a controlled invocation path.

Options:
1. Use system `sqlite3` binary (fast to start, but version drift).
2. Build `sqlite3` from `legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite` submodule (preferred for reproducibility).
3. Link libsqlite via rusqlite/libsqlite3-sys (best embedding; adds deps).

## Acceptance
- Harness can run a SQL script against a DB file and capture:
  - stdout rows (if any)
  - error code/message
  - wall time
- The sqlite version is recorded in report metadata.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:21.160448746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:05:33.683978680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:05:33.683956459Z","close_reason":"Baseline (C SQLite oracle) executors implemented: rusqlite-backed OpLog runner in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/sqlite_executor.rs + sqlite3-CLI multi-process runner/report in crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/executor.rs (captures stdout/stderr snippets, busy/error counts, sqlite3 version). realdb-e2e run --engine sqlite3 emits JSON with rusqlite version. Gates: fmt/check/clippy/test green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","harness","sqlite"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:21.160448746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:22:19.327079314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1079,"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:33Z"}]}
